back_translation: And it happened after this, that Absalom prepared for himself a chariot and horses, and fifty men running before him.

And Absalom rose early and stood by the way of the gate; and it happened that every man who had a dispute to come to the king for judgment, Absalom called to him and said, 'From what city are you?' And he said, 'From one of the tribes of Israel is your servant.'
And Absalom said to him, 'See, your words are good and right, but no one will hear you from the king.'
And Absalom said, 'Who would appoint me judge in the land, and to me would come every man who has a dispute and judgment, and I would give him justice.'
And it would happen, when any man approached to bow to him, that he would put forth his hand and take hold of him and kiss him.
And Absalom did according to this word to all Israel who came for judgment to the king; and Absalom stole the heart of the men of Israel.
And it happened at the end of four years, that Absalom said to the king, 'Let me please go and pay my vow that I have vowed to the LORD in Hebron.
For your servant vowed a vow while I was dwelling in Geshur in Aram, saying, If the LORD will indeed bring me back to Jerusalem, then I will serve the LORD.'
And the king said to him, 'Go in peace.' And he arose and went to Hebron.
And Absalom sent spies throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, 'When you hear the sound of the horn, you shall say, Absalom reigns as king in Hebron.'
And with Absalom went two hundred men from Jerusalem, invited and going in their innocence — they knew nothing at all.

And Absalom sent for Ahithophel the Gilonite, David's counselor, from his city, from Giloh, while he was offering the sacrifices; and the conspiracy was strong, and the people went on, increasing with Absalom.
And the messenger came to David, saying, 'The heart of the men of Israel is after Absalom.'

And David said to all his servants who were with him in Jerusalem, 'Arise and let us flee; for we shall have no escape from before Absalom. Hurry to go, lest he hasten and overtake us and bring upon us evil and strike the city by the mouth of the sword.'
And the king's servants said to the king, 'According to all that my lord the king chooses, behold your servants.'
And the king went out, and all his house on foot; and the king left ten women, concubines, to keep the house.
And the king went out and all the people on foot, and they stood at the house of the distance.

And all his servants crossed over by his hand, and all the Cherethites and all the Pelethites, and all the Gittites — six hundred men who had come on foot from Gath — crossed over before the face of the king.
And the king said to Ittai the Gittite, 'Why should you also go with us? Return and stay with the king, for you are a foreigner and also an exile to your place.
Yesterday was your coming, and today shall I move you with us to go? — and I am going where I am going. Return and bring back your brothers with you in steadfast love and truth.'

And Ittai answered the king and said, 'As the LORD lives, and as my lord the king lives — surely in the place where my lord the king shall be, whether for death or for life, surely there shall your servant be.'
And David said to Ittai, 'Go and cross over.' And Ittai the Gittite crossed over, and all his men, and all the little ones who were with him.
And all the land was weeping with a great voice, and all the people were crossing over; and the king crossed over the brook Kidron, and all the people crossed over toward the way of the wilderness.

And behold, also Zadok, and all the Levites with him bearing the ark of the covenant of God; and they set down the ark of God, and Abiathar went up until all the people had finished crossing from the city.
And the king said to Zadok, 'Return the ark of God to the city. If I find favor in the eyes of the LORD, then He will bring me back and let me see it and His dwelling place.
But if thus He shall say, I have no delight in you — here I am, let Him do to me as is good in His eyes.'
And the king said to Zadok the priest, 'Are you a seer? Return to the city in peace, and Ahimaaz your son and Jonathan son of Abiathar, the two of your sons with you.'
'See, I will be waiting in the plains of the wilderness until a word comes from you to tell me.'
And Zadok and Abiathar returned the ark of God to Jerusalem, and they stayed there.

And David went up by the ascent of the olives, going up and weeping, and his head was covered, and he was walking barefoot; and all the people who were with him each covered his head, and they went up, going up and weeping.
And David was told, saying, 'Ahithophel is among the conspirators with Absalom.' And David said, 'Please turn into foolishness the counsel of Ahithophel, O LORD.'
And it happened, when David came to the summit where he used to bow to God, that behold, Hushai the Archite was there to meet him, with his coat torn and dust on his head.
And David said to him, 'If you cross over with me, then you will be a burden upon me.

But if you return to the city and say to Absalom, I am your servant, O king; I was the servant of your father from that time, and now I am your servant — then you shall break for me the counsel of Ahithophel.
And are not Zadok and Abiathar the priests with you there? And every word that you hear from the house of the king you shall tell to Zadok and to Abiathar the priests.
Behold, there with them are two of their sons, Ahimaaz of Zadok and Jonathan of Abiathar; and you shall send to me by their hand every word that you hear.'
And Hushai the friend of David came to the city, and Absalom was coming to Jerusalem.
